title: Consumer/partner outreach + Zenodo fallback path
context: The project's Definition of Shipped requires a real beneficiary: a corpus that is produced but not used or accepted fails the Hee-Lee Oss 'delivered, not merged' standard. This outreach task ensures M0 does not conclude with an unused corpus. The self-serve Zenodo DOI fallback means M0 can yield a citable, accepted outcome even without a named partner — but finding a real consumer (lab/advocacy/NLP group) is preferred. Until a consumer is confirmed, all delivery-dependent tasks carry verifiedNeed:false.
objective: Open ≥ 1 partner outreach thread with a candidate consumer (academic cancer-informatics lab, patient-advocacy research program, biomedical-NLP community, or BioNLP/Europe PMC ecosystem), document the outreach and responses, and establish the Zenodo fallback release path as a concrete alternative if no partner confirms by the M0 pilot release.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 1 partner-outreach thread is opened with a named candidate consumer (lab, advocacy org, or NLP group), with the initial message and contact recorded in a committed document.
The document records the candidate's response (or lack thereof by a defined deadline), the proposed consumption mechanism, and the next step.
The Zenodo fallback path is documented: a step-by-step plan for publishing a versioned Zenodo record with a DOI, including the license, metadata fields, and the Steward responsible for submission.
The document states which path (informal partner or Zenodo self-serve) will be used for the M0 pilot release, and why.
All outreach messages are honest about the project's current state (planning stage, no named partner yet, verifiedNeed:false) and do not overstate readiness.
resources: PLAN.md (Problem & beneficiaries; Cold-start de-risking; Partner org section)
Europe PMC ecosystem / BioNLP community contacts
output: A committed document recording all partner outreach threads, responses, the chosen M0 release path (partner or Zenodo), and the Zenodo fallback procedure — to be updated as outreach progresses and referenced by pmc-cancer-partner-011.
